frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: At work it's such a strange double standard, no? If a woman comes well turned out, people immediately say she is wearing too much makeup and trying too hard. But if she shows up plain for a few days, suddenly it's 'she has stopped taking care of herself' — as if there is some perfect natural setting we are all supposed to maintain.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: This is not endorsing the misogynistic belief; it is criticizing the double bind women face around appearance. It references the frame's surface manifestations ('too much makeup' vs 'not taking care of herself') in order to reject them.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
